devanagari: अथ तृतीयोऽध्यायः । श्रीनारद उवाच । हिरण्यकशिपू राजन्नजेयमजरामरम् । आत्मानमप्रतिद्वन्द्वमेकराजं व्यधित्सत ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: atha tṛtīyo śrīnārada uvāca hiraṇyakaśipū rājannajeyamajarāmaram ātmānamapratidvandvamekarājaṃ vyadhitsata

translation:
Nārada said:
Oh King (Yudhiṣṭhira)! Hiraṇyakaśipu wished to be invincible, unaffected by old age and death, unrivalled and the absolute ruler of all the worlds.

devanagari: स तेपे मन्दरद्रोण्यां तपः परमदारुणम् । ऊर्ध्वबाहुर्नभोदृष्टिः पादाङ्गुष्ठाश्रितावनिः ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: sa tepe mandaradroṇyāṃ tapaḥ paramadāruṇam ūrdhvabāhurnabhodṛṣṭiḥ pādāṅguṣṭhāśritāvaniḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: With his arms stretched upwards, eyes fixed in the sky and (all the while) standing on the tips of his big toes, Hiraṇyakaśipu (with the abovementioned desire) performed penance of a severe type, in the valley of mount Mandara.

devanagari: जटादीधितिभी रेजे संवर्तार्क इवांशुभिः । तस्मिंस्तपस्तप्यमाने देवाः स्थानानि भेजिरे ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: jaṭādīdhitibhī reje saṃvartārka ivāṃśubhiḥ tasmiṃstapastapyamāne devāḥ sthānāni bhejire

translation: Like the Sun radiating fiery rays at the time of the destruction of the world, he shone with the brilliant splendour of his matted hair. While he was thus engaged in austerities, gods (who disappeared out of fear) returned to their (former) abodes (in heaven).

devanagari: तस्य मूर्ध्नः समुद्भूतः सधूमोऽग्निस्तपोमयः । तिर्यगूर्ध्वमधो लोकान्प्रातपद्विष्वगीरितः ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: tasya mūrdhnaḥ samudbhūtaḥ sadhūmo tiryagūrdhvamadho lokānprātapadviṣvagīritaḥ
translation: As a result of his (severe) penance, smoky fire emanated from the crown of his head. It spread in all directions and scorched the higher, middle and lower regions of the universe.

devanagari: चुक्षुभुर्नद्युदन्वन्तः सद्वीपाद्रि श्चचाल भूः । निपेतुः सग्रहास्तारा जज्वलुश्च दिशो दश ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: cukṣubhurnadyudanvantaḥ sadvīpādri ścacāla bhūḥ nipetuḥ sagrahāstārā jajvaluśca diśo daśa
translation:
Rivers and oceans were agitated; the earth along with its continents and mountains quaked (violently); planets and stars fell down (from their orbits); all the ten directions were ablaze.
6-7 Being scorched (with that fire), gods deserted the heaven and went to the region of god Brahmā, and implored to the creator of the world: “Oh god of gods, Lord of the universe! Being burnt (severely afflicted) with the penance of Hiraṇyakaśipu, the king of Daityas, we are unable to stay in the Heaven. Oh great god! If it pleases you, please bring about its pacification (extinction) before all the people (or regions) who worship you, are not destroyed, Oh Supreme Lord!

devanagari: तेन तप्ता दिवं त्यक्त्वा ब्रह्मलोकं ययुः सुराः । धात्रे विज्ञापयामासुर्देवदेव जगत्पते ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: tena taptā divaṃ tyaktvā brahmalokaṃ yayuḥ surāḥ dhātre vijñāpayāmāsurdevadeva jagatpate

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ tap class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ tap class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ tap class 4 verb], [vocative plural from √ tap class 4 verb]

devanagari: दैत्येन्द्र तपसा तप्ता दिवि स्थातुं न शक्नुमः । तस्य चोपशमं भूमन्विधेहि यदि मन्यसे ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: daityendra tapasā taptā divi sthātuṃ na śaknumaḥ tasya copaśamaṃ bhūmanvidhehi yadi manyase

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ tap class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ tap class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ tap class 4 verb], [vocative plural from √ tap class 4 verb]

[present middle first single], [imperative active second single]
[present middle second single], [present passive second single]

devanagari: लोका न यावन्नङ्क्ष्यन्ति बलिहारास्तवाभिभूः । तस्यायं किल सङ्कल्पश्चरतो दुश्चरं तपः ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: lokā na yāvannaṅkṣyanti balihārāstavābhibhūḥ tasyāyaṃ kila saṅkalpaścarato duścaraṃ tapaḥ
translation:
Do you not know his intention of performing this severely austere penance? (If not) be pleased to listen to it as reported by us.
9-10 ‘Having created this universe of mobiles and immobiles by dint of his penance and Yogic meditation, the creator of the universe occupies his throne (position) exalted over all regions. I too will attain a similar high position for me, by my ever-increasing devotion to asceticism and Yogic concentration. For Time and Soul are eternal.

devanagari: श्रूयतां किं न विदितस्तवाथापि निवेदितम् । सृष्ट्वा चराचरमिदं तपोयोगसमाधिना । अध्यास्ते सर्वधिष्ण्येभ्यः परमेष्ठी निजासनम् ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: śrūyatāṃ kiṃ na viditastavāthāpi niveditam sṛṣṭvā carācaramidaṃ tapoyogasamādhinā adhyāste sarvadhiṣṇyebhyaḥ parameṣṭhī nijāsanam

devanagari: तदहं वर्धमानेन तपोयोगसमाधिना । कालात्मनोश्च नित्यत्वात्साधयिष्ये तथात्मनः ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: tadahaṃ vardhamānena tapoyogasamādhinā kālātmanośca nityatvātsādhayiṣye tathātmanaḥ

devanagari: अन्यथेदं विधास्येऽहमयथा पूर्वमोजसा । किमन्यैः कालनिर्धूतैः कल्पान्ते वैष्णवादिभिः ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: anyathedaṃ vidhāsye kimanyaiḥ kālanirdhūtaiḥ kalpānte vaiṣṇavādibhiḥ
translation: Otherwise, by the power of my penance, I shall reverse the old order of the universe and make it as it was not before (making sins result in happiness, meritorious deeds lead to miseries. The Asuras will be exalted to Svarga and gods and sages driven to Pātāla). What have I to do with the positions of Dhruva, Viṣṇu and others which are abolished by Time, at the end of a Kalpa?[1]
purport:
Footnote [1]:
The realm of Viṣṇu is eternal. But Hiraṇyakaśipu being of demonic and Tāmasa intellect thought it to be perishable.

devanagari: इति शुश्रुम निर्बन्धं तपः परममास्थितः । विधत्स्वानन्तरं युक्तं स्वयं त्रिभुवनेश्वर ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: iti śuśruma nirbandhaṃ tapaḥ paramamāsthitaḥ vidhatsvānantaraṃ yuktaṃ svayaṃ tribhuvaneśvara

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ yuj class 7 verb], [accusative single from √ yuj class 7 verb]

translation: We heard it reported that, it is with that determination, he (Hiraṇyakaśipu) undertook those rigorous religious austerities. Oh Lord of the three worlds! Now you may please do promptly whatever is proper.

devanagari: तवासनं द्विजगवां पारमेष्ठ्यं जगत्पते । भवाय श्रेयसे भूत्यै क्षेमाय विजयाय च ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: tavāsanaṃ dvijagavāṃ pārameṣṭhyaṃ jagatpate bhavāya śreyase bhūtyai kṣemāya vijayāya ca
translation: Your exalted position as Parameṣṭhī promotes the growth, welfare, prosperity, good fortune and victory of the twice-born ones and cows. Oh Lord of the universe.”

devanagari: इति विज्ञापितो देवैर्भगवानात्मभूर्नृप । परितो भृगुदक्षाद्यैर्ययौ दैत्येश्वराश्रमम् ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: iti vijñāpito devairbhagavānātmabhūrnṛpa parito bhṛgudakṣādyairyayau daityeśvarāśramam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Thus respectfully requested by gods, Brahmā, the glorious, self-originated god, accompanied by Bhṛgu, Dakṣa and others, went to the hermitage of the king of Daityas.

devanagari: न ददर्श प्रतिच्छन्नं वल्मीकतृणकीचकैः । पिपीलिकाभिराचीर्णं मेदस्त्वङ्मांसशोणितम् ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: na dadarśa praticchannaṃ valmīkatṛṇakīcakaiḥ pipīlikābhirācīrṇaṃ medastvaṅmāṃsaśoṇitam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: But (at first) they could not find him as he was covered with an ant hill, grass and bamboos and as his skin, fat, flesh, and blood were eaten up on all sides, by ants.

devanagari: तपन्तं तपसा लोकान्यथाभ्रापिहितं रविम् । विलक्ष्य विस्मितः प्राह हसंस्तं हंसवाहनः ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: tapantaṃ tapasā lokānyathābhrāpihitaṃ ravim vilakṣya vismitaḥ prāha hasaṃstaṃ haṃsavāhanaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ has class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ has class 1 verb]

translation: Brahmā (the swan-riding god) was surprised on seeing Hiraṇyakaśipu tormenting the world with his religious austerities (while lying concealed thus) like the Sun (scorching the world though) covered with clouds. Laughing aloud, he spoke:

devanagari: श्रीब्रह्मोवाच । उत्तिष्ठोत्तिष्ठ भद्रं ते तपःसिद्धोऽसि काश्यप । वरदोऽहमनुप्राप्तो व्रियतामीप्सितो वरः ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: śrībrahmovāca uttiṣṭhottiṣṭha bhadraṃ te tapaḥsiddho varado

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Brahmā said:
Arise, arise, Oh Son of Kaśyapa! May good betide you! You have accomplished the purpose of your penance. I, the bestower of boons, have come here (to confer any boon you ask of me). Ask for any boon desired by you.

devanagari: अद्रा क्षमहमेतं ते हृत्सारं महदद्भुतम् । दंशभक्षितदेहस्य प्राणा ह्यस्थिषु शेरते ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: adrā kṣamahametaṃ te hṛtsāraṃ mahadadbhutam daṃśabhakṣitadehasya prāṇā hyasthiṣu śerate

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: I have seen your extremely wonderful fortitude and patience. Though your body is eaten up by gnats, your life resides in your bony skeleton only.

devanagari: नैतत्पूर्वर्षयश्चक्रुर्न करिष्यन्ति चापरे । निरम्बुर्धारयेत्प्राणान्को वै दिव्यसमाः शतम् ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: naitatpūrvarṣayaścakrurna kariṣyanti cāpare niramburdhārayetprāṇānko vai divyasamāḥ śatam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [accusative plural from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [nominative plural from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [vocative plural from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [accusative plural from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [nominative plural from √ kṛ class 8 verb], [vocative plural from √ kṛ class 8 verb], [accusative plural from √ kṛ class 8 verb]

[vocative single from √ kṝ class 5 verb], [vocative single from √ kṝ class 9 verb]
[present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]

translation: None of the sages of yore has performed such austere penance, nor shall others do it in future. Who can verily sustain his life without (drinking a drop of) water for a hundred celestial years?[2]
purport:
Footnote [2]:
Human year of 360 days corresponds to I day of gods. Thus, one celestial year = 360 human years. 100 celestial years = 36,000 human years.

devanagari: व्यवसायेन तेऽनेन दुष्करेण मनस्विनाम् । तपोनिष्ठेन भवताजितोऽहं दितिनन्दन ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: vyavasāyena te taponiṣṭhena bhavatājito
translation: By this resoluteness of yours which is hard to practise even by those (great sages) who control their mind, and by your firmness in austere penance, I am conquered by you, Oh son of Diti.

devanagari: ततस्त आशिषः सर्वा ददाम्यसुरपुङ्गव । मर्तस्य ते ह्यमर्तस्य दर्शनं नाफलं मम ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: tatasta āśiṣaḥ sarvā dadāmyasurapuṅgava martasya te hyamartasya darśanaṃ nāphalaṃ mama
translation: I shall bestow on you, Oh leader of Asuras, all the blessings. The sight of an immortal god like me will not be unfructuous and in vain for you (who are but) a mortal.

devanagari: श्रीनारद उवाच । इ त्युक्त्वादिभवो देवो भक्षिताङ्गं पिपीलिकैः । कमण्डलुजलेनौक्षद्दिव्येनामोघराधसा ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīnārada uvāca i tyuktvādibhavo devo bhakṣitāṅgaṃ pipīlikaiḥ kamaṇḍalujalenaukṣaddivyenāmogharādhasā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Nārada said:
Having spoken to him (Hiraṇyakaśipu) thus, the first-born god Brahmā sprinkled on the body (of Hiraṇyakaśipu) which was eaten up by ants, celestial water from his Kamaṇḍalu (earthen or wooden water pot) charged with unfailing potency.

devanagari: स तत्कीचकवल्मीकात्सहओजोबलान्वितः । सर्वावयवसम्पन्नो वज्रसंहननो युवा । उत्थितस्तप्तहेमाभो विभावसुरिवैधसः ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: sa tatkīcakavalmīkātsahaojobalānvitaḥ sarvāvayavasampanno vajrasaṃhanano yuvā utthitastaptahemābho vibhāvasurivaidhasaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect active second single], [imperfect active third single]

translation: From the ant-hill covered with bamboos, there arose, like fire from fuel, a youth of adamantine frame of the body, perfect in all limbs, endowed with energy and power, and with complexion like molten gold.

devanagari: स निरीक्ष्याम्बरे देवं हंसवाहमुपस्थितम् । ननाम शिरसा भूमौ तद्दर्शनमहोत्सवः ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: sa nirīkṣyāmbare devaṃ haṃsavāhamupasthitam nanāma śirasā bhūmau taddarśanamahotsavaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Finding god Brahmā seated on his swan, and stationed in the sky, he was transported with joy at this sight, and reverentially touched the ground with his head.

devanagari: उत्थाय प्राञ्जलिः प्रह्व ईक्षमाणो दृशा विभुम् । हर्षाश्रुपुलकोद्भेदो गिरा गद्गदयागृणात् ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: utthāya prāñjaliḥ prahva īkṣamāṇo dṛśā vibhum harṣāśrupulakodbhedo girā gadgadayāgṛṇāt
translation: Rising up, he saw the god Brahmā with his own eyes. Reverentially he folded his hands in a suppliant position. With his eyes full of tears of joy, and with his hair standing on their ends, he expressed the following prayer with his voice choked with tears of joy.

devanagari: श्रीहिरण्यकशिपुरुवाच । कल्पान्ते कालसृष्टेन योऽन्धेन तमसावृतम् । अभिव्यनग्जगदिदं स्वयञ्ज्योतिः स्वरोचिषा ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīhiraṇyakaśipuruvāca kalpānte kālasṛṣṭena yo abhivyanagjagadidaṃ svayañjyotiḥ svarociṣā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Hiraṇyakaśipu said:
[3] “Bow to the all-pervading Supreme Lord, the support of the guṇas—sattva, rajas and tamas, who, being self- luminous, manifested by his own splendour (power), this universe which was enveloped in intense tamas (not darkness), produced by Time, at the time of the periodic destruction of the universe (at the end of each Kalpa), and who, assuming the three guṇas of Prakṛti creates, protects and destroys this universe.
purport:
Footnote [3]:
Bhāgavata Candrikā notes that due to the tāmasa nature of Hiraṇyakaśipu, he wrongly thinks god Brahmā to be the Almighty Lord. Padaratnāvalī also points out that the attributes used here are inapplicable to the fourfaced god Brahmā but are proper for Hari who, as an antaryāmin, dwells in all and controls Brahmā.

devanagari: आत्मना त्रिवृता चेदं सृजत्यवति लुम्पति । रजःसत्त्वतमोधाम्ने पराय महते नमः ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: ātmanā trivṛtā cedaṃ sṛjatyavati lumpati rajaḥsattvatamodhāmne parāya mahate namaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ sṛj class 6 verb], [vocative dual from √ sṛj class 6 verb], [accusative dual from √ sṛj class 6 verb], [locative single from √ sṛj class 6 verb]

translation:
Hiraṇyakaśipu said:
[3] “Bow to the all-pervading Supreme Lord, the support of the guṇas—sattva, rajas and tamas, who, being self- luminous, manifested by his own splendour (power), this universe which was enveloped in intense tamas (not darkness), produced by Time, at the time of the periodic destruction of the universe (at the end of each Kalpa), and who, assuming the three guṇas of Prakṛti creates, protects and destroys this universe.
purport:
Footnote [3]:
Bhāgavata Candrikā notes that due to the tāmasa nature of Hiraṇyakaśipu, he wrongly thinks god Brahmā to be the Almighty Lord. Padaratnāvalī also points out that the attributes used here are inapplicable to the fourfaced god Brahmā but are proper for Hari who, as an antaryāmin, dwells in all and controls Brahmā.

devanagari: नम आद्याय बीजाय ज्ञानविज्ञानमूर्तये । प्राणेन्द्रि यमनोबुद्धि विकारैर्व्यक्तिमीयुषे ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: nama ādyāya bījāya jñānavijñānamūrtaye prāṇendri yamanobuddhi vikārairvyaktimīyuṣe
translation: [4] Salutations to the Primary Cause (the instrumental cause of the universe who is knowledge and wisdom incarnate, and who (as the material cause) becomes manifest through the modifications, viz., vital airs (prāṇa), senses (indriya), mind and intellect.
purport:
Footnote [4]:
Padaratnāvalī shows that the attributes mentioned herein are applicable both to the Supreme Lord and to god Brahmā.

devanagari: त्वमीशिषे जगतस्तस्थुषश्च प्राणेन मुख्येन पतिः प्रजानाम् । चित्तस्य चित्तैर्मनैन्द्रि याणां पतिर्महान्भूतगुणाशयेशः ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: tvamīśiṣe jagatastasthuṣaśca prāṇena mukhyena patiḥ prajānām cittasya cittairmanaindri yāṇāṃ patirmahānbhūtaguṇāśayeśaḥ
translation: You govern the mobiles and immobiles through the chief Prāṇa (chief of the five vital airs) and hence, you are the protector of all the creatures, the controller of the mind, and life principles, of the heart and the sense-organs. You are the mahat (and) as such the ruler of the elements (bhūtas), their guṇas (objects such as sound, touch etc.) as well as their vāsanās (sense-impressions).

devanagari: त्वं सप्ततन्तून्वितनोषि तन्वा त्रय्या चतुर्होत्रकविद्यया च । त्वमेक आत्मात्मवतामनादिरनन्तपारः कविरन्तरात्मा ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: tvaṃ saptatantūnvitanoṣi tanvā trayyā caturhotrakavidyayā ca tvameka ātmātmavatāmanādiranantapāraḥ kavirantarātmā
translation: Through your body consisting of the three Vedas, and the sacrificial lore, requiring four officiating priests (viz. Hotṛ, Adhvaryu, Udgātṛ and Brahmā), you perform the seven types of sacrifices (such as Agniṣṭoma, Atyagniṣṭoma, Uktha, Ṣoḍaśī, Atirātra, Āptoryāma and Vājapeya). You are the Soul of all living beings. You are indivisible, without a beginning and end, and unlimited by time and place, the Omniscient Indwelling Soul and Controller.

devanagari: त्वमेव कालोऽनिमिषो जनानामायुर्लवाद्यवयवैः क्षिणोषि । कूटस्थ आत्मा परमेष्ठ्यजो महांस्त्वं जीवलोकस्य च जीव आत्मा ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: tvameva kālo kūṭastha ātmā parameṣṭhyajo mahāṃstvaṃ jīvalokasya ca jīva ātmā
translation: You alone are the unwinking Time. You reduce the duration of the life of men by units of time called lava, nimeṣa and others. You are the immutable Soul, the occupant of the most exalted position, the birthless, all-pervading principle, the supporter and controller of all living beings.

devanagari: त्वत्तः परं नापरमप्यनेजदेजच्च किञ्चिद्व्यतिरिक्तमस्ति । विद्याः कलास्ते तनवश्च सर्वा हिरण्यगर्भोऽसि बृहत्त्रिपृष्ठः ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: tvattaḥ paraṃ nāparamapyanejadejacca kiñcidvyatiriktamasti vidyāḥ kalāste tanavaśca sarvā hiraṇyagarbho

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ bṛh ], [vocative single from √ bṛh ], [accusative single from √ bṛh ]
[nominative single from √ bṛh ], [vocative single from √ bṛh ], [accusative single from √ bṛh ]

translation: [5] Neither cause nor effect, or neither mobiles nor immobiles are apart from you. All the branches of knowledge (viz. Vedas and upa-Vedas) and arts (auxiliaries of Vedic studies e.g. phonetics, grammar, prosody etc.) it, this shining are your forms. You are the great Brahmā containing within egg of the universe. You are beyond (transcendental to) Prakṛti consisting of three guṇas.
purport:
Footnote [5]:
Padaratnāvalī points out that the attributes in this verse are applicable to the Supreme Lord and this verse is a eulogy of the Inner Controller (antaryāmin).
“No mobiles and immobiles of the past, present and future can stand outside and unsupported by you. Both the parā and aparā vidyās abide in your body and are the means of knowing you. You are perfect and beyond the reach of the three stages, viz. wakefulness, sleep and dream.

devanagari: व्यक्तं विभो स्थूलमिदं शरीरं येनेन्द्रि यप्राणमनोगुणांस्त्वम् । भुङ्क्षे स्थितो धामनि पारमेष्ठ्ये अव्यक्त आत्मा पुरुषः पुराणः ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: vyaktaṃ vibho sthūlamidaṃ śarīraṃ yenendri yaprāṇamanoguṇāṃstvam bhuṅkṣe sthito dhāmani pārameṣṭhye avyakta ātmā puruṣaḥ purāṇaḥ
translation: [6] Oh all-pervading Lord! This gross manifested universe is your body through which you enjoy the objects of senses, life-breath (vital energy) and the mind, remaining all the while established in your original most exalted state. You are the unmanifest (subtle) ātman and the most ancient Supreme Person.
purport:
Footnote [6]:
This verse sums up both the gross and subtle forms of Brahmā.—Bhāgavata Candrikā
Padaratnāvalī.—The words hiraṇya-garbha etc. show that the universe is Hari’s gross form, in which he enjoys supreme bliss and not misery, or pain like Devadatta (an ordinary man).
As usual Padaratnāvalī gives two interpretations, one applicable to Hari and the other to Brahmā—
(1) “Oh Hari, you are the most ancient person in Vaikuṇṭha. This gross manifested universe” etc.
(2) “Oh god Brahmā, you are the Soul (purāṇa—one bound to the body) the four-faced god who does “not incarnate (avyakta)”, the rest as above.

devanagari: अनन्ताव्यक्तरूपेण येनेदमखिलं ततम् । चिदचिच्छक्तियुक्ताय तस्मै भगवते नमः ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: anantāvyaktarūpeṇa yenedamakhilaṃ tatam cidacicchaktiyuktāya tasmai bhagavate namaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]
[nominative single from √ tan class 8 verb], [accusative single from √ tan class 8 verb]

translation: Salutation to the glorious Lord who has pervaded the whole of the universe by His infinite, unmanifest form and who is possessed of powers called cit or vidyā and acit or māyā.

devanagari: यदि दास्यस्यभिमतान्वरान्मे वरदोत्तम । भूतेभ्यस्त्वद्विसृष्टेभ्यो मृत्युर्मा भून्मम प्रभो ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: yadi dāsyasyabhimatānvarānme varadottama bhūtebhyastvadvisṛṣṭebhyo mṛtyurmā bhūnmama prabho
translation: Oh greatest among the bestowers of boons! If you are to confer on me my desired boons, may my death not take place at the hands of any being created by You, Oh Lord.

devanagari: नान्तर्बहिर्दिवा नक्तमन्यस्मादपि चायुधैः । न भूमौ नाम्बरे मृत्युर्न नरैर्न मृगैरपि ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: nāntarbahirdivā naktamanyasmādapi cāyudhaiḥ na bhūmau nāmbare mṛtyurna narairna mṛgairapi

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ naj class 6 verb], [accusative single from √ naj class 6 verb]

translation: Let me not meet death indoors or out of doors; during night or at day time, by the hand of any other (being created by you) or by means of weapons; let there be no death to me either on the earth or in the sky, either by men or beasts; by gods, demons and big serpents; by beings whether living or dead. Grant me matchlessness in fight and the sole absolute rulership of all embodied beings.

devanagari: व्यसुभिर्वासुमद्भिर्वा सुरासुरमहोरगैः । अप्रतिद्वन्द्वतां युद्धे ऐकपत्यं च देहिनाम् ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: vyasubhirvāsumadbhirvā surāsuramahoragaiḥ apratidvandvatāṃ yuddhe aikapatyaṃ ca dehinām

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [locative single from √ yudh class 1 verb], [locative single from √ yudh class 4 verb]

[nominative dual from √ yudh class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ yudh class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ yudh class 1 verb], [locative single from √ yudh class 1 verb], [nominative dual from √ yudh class 4 verb], [vocative dual from √ yudh class 4 verb], [accusative dual from √ yudh class 4 verb], [locative single from √ yudh class 4 verb]

[nominative dual from √ yudh class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ yudh class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ yudh class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ yudh class 1 verb], [nominative dual from √ yudh class 4 verb], [vocative single from √ yudh class 4 verb], [vocative dual from √ yudh class 4 verb], [accusative dual from √ yudh class 4 verb]

translation: Let me not meet death indoors or out of doors; during night or at day time, by the hand of any other (being created by you) or by means of weapons; let there be no death to me either on the earth or in the sky, either by men or beasts; by gods, demons and big serpents; by beings whether living or dead. Grant me matchlessness in fight and the sole absolute rulership of all embodied beings.

devanagari: सर्वेषां लोकपालानां महिमानं यथात्मनः । तपोयोगप्रभावाणां यन्न रिष्यति कर्हिचित् ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: sarveṣāṃ lokapālānāṃ mahimānaṃ yathātmanaḥ tapoyogaprabhāvāṇāṃ yanna riṣyati karhicit

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation:
(Lastly) confer on me the great position and power you enjoy among all the guardians of the world. Kindly grant me the undiminishing glory and unfailing mystical powers enjoyed by those whose power lies in asceticism and Yoga.”
